title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Dry Skin
intro: Herbal teas for dry skin are a natural remedy made from infusions of herbs, flowers, and plants that are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, used to soothe and moisturize dry skin. These teas can treat dry skin by providing hydration from the inside out, reducing inflammation, and promoting skin elasticity. Examples of herbal teas that can help alleviate dry skin include peppermint tea, which cools and calms the skin, chamomile tea, which soothes and relaxes the skin, green tea, which is rich in antioxidants that protect the skin from damage, licorice root tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation, and calendula tea, which promotes skin healing and regeneration. Additionally, rosehip tea, hibiscus tea, and ginger tea are also beneficial in treating dry skin due to their high levels of vitamins and minerals that help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from environmental stressors.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dry skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and emollient properties. The bioactive constituents, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids, help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. By using Calendula officinalis teas, individuals can experience relief from dry skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as improve skin elasticity and hydration. The benefits of this herbal preparation include reduced redness, itching, and irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting healthy and nourished skin.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers or 1 tablespoon of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan until it starts boiling.
Remove the water from heat and add the Calendula flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the flowers.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Roumanian archives of microbiology and immunology", Calendula officinalis teas for dry skin may be beneficial due to its antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote skin health.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat dry skin ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals. To use Calendula officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to patch test the tea on a small area of skin before applying it to the affected area, and to start with a low concentration to gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it on open wounds or sensitive areas.
preparation_usage: Wash dry skin with mild soap and lukewarm water.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Calendula officinalis in 1 cup boiling water.
Let the tea cool for 10-15 minutes to reduce temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply to affected skin.
Use 2-3 times a day, and before bed for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of nettle tea, including flavonoids, carotenoids, and phenolic acids, help to soothe and moisturize dry skin, reducing inflammation and promoting skin health. By consuming nettle tea, individuals can experience relief from dry skin symptoms, such as itching and flakiness, and promote a more hydrated and healthy complexion. The benefits of using nettle tea to treat dry skin include its natural and non-irritating properties, making it a great alternative to harsh commercial treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for taste.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Frontiers in pharmacology", Urtica dioica teas for dry skin show potential benefits, but there is no specific information in the provided study about the use of Urtica dioica teas for dry skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be a potential remedy for dry skin, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, or increased heart rate in some individuals. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid consuming it if you have open wounds or sensitive skin, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried Urtica dioica leaves as a supplement daily.
Soak in a Urtica dioica tea bath for 15-20 minutes, 2-3 times a week.
Apply Urtica dioica tea compress to dry skin areas 2-3 times a week.
Use Urtica dioica creams or ointments as directed on the product label.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat dry skin ailments due to their soothing and moisturizing properties, which help to calm and hydrate the skin. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as vitamins A, C, and E, as well as glycoproteins and polysaccharides, that help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Aloe barbadensis teas enable it to treat dry skin conditions by promoting skin elasticity and preventing further dryness. Regular consumption of Aloe barbadensis teas has been found to provide numerous benefits, including improved skin hydration, reduced redness and irritation, and enhanced skin elasticity, making it an effective natural remedy for dry skin ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 2-3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Cut the aloe vera leaf and extract the clear gel inside.
Combine the aloe vera gel with the cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink while warm, 1-2 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be an effective treatment for dry skin ailments, but it may cause some side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Aloe barbadensis teas safely, one should be cautious of its interactions with other medications, avoid taking it in excess, and monitor their skin for any signs of irritation or sensitivity.
preparation_usage: Soak a cloth in 1 cup of cooled Aloe barbadensis tea.
Apply the cloth to dry skin areas for 15-30 minutes.
Use 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis tea as a skin cream.
Apply the tea mixture 2-3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Repeat the process as needed, but not more than 2 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm teas, have been used for centuries to treat dry skin ailments due to their soothing and moisturizing properties. These herbal preparations help to treat dry skin by reducing inflammation, promoting hydration, and calming irritated skin, thus providing relief from itching and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, such as rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and volatile oils, play a crucial role in its therapeutic effects, as they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to repair and protect the skin. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dry skin include improved skin hydration, reduced inflammation, and a calming effect that promotes overall skin health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a strong tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day to soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Melissa officinalis teas for dry skin may be beneficial due to the decoction's ability to reestablish skin physiology by decreasing dryness and enhancing skin barrier function.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat dry skin ailments, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased sensitivity to the sun due to its phototoxic properties. When using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dry skin, it's essential to take precautions like avoiding prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, wearing protective clothing, and applying sunscreen with a high SPF to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day
Take 1 tablespoon of Melissa officinalis extract 2 times a day
Use 5-10 drops of Melissa officinalis essential oil in bath water
Soak in bath for 15-20 minutes 1-2 times a week
Repeat for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable improvements in skin

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat dry skin ailments, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and promote skin health. By reducing the severity of dry skin symptoms and promoting wound healing, Echinacea purpurea teas provide relief from discomfort and irritation associated with dry skin. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat dry skin ailments include reduced inflammation, improved skin barrier function, and enhanced overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat dry skin, but they may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, dizziness, and nausea in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or over an extended period. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming them if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and also to keep them out of reach of children and pets.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 cup of boiling water
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes
Drink 1-2 cups daily for 2-3 weeks
Monitor and adjust dosage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been used for centuries as a natural remedy to treat dry skin ailments such as eczema and dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promote skin hydration, thereby alleviating dry skin symptoms. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can provide long-term benefits, including improved skin health and reduced risk of skin infections.

Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and add it to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the powder and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the powder and discard the solids.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 1-2 times a day, to help soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat dry skin ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of low potassium levels, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your potassium levels regularly, and avoid consuming it with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day
Take 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root daily
Use it for 4-6 weeks for optimal skin improvement
Combine with moisturizers for enhanced dry skin relief
Monitor skin condition and adjust usage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been used to treat dry skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The bioactive constituents, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiseptic effects, which aid in healing and protecting the skin. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat dry skin include reduced inflammation, improved skin hydration, and a faster recovery time.

Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried flowers in the infuser.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be beneficial in treating dry skin, but some users may experience side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to the high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin. The benefits of using Chamomilla recutita teas to treat dry skin include improved skin hydration, reduced inflammation, and enhanced skin elasticity, making it a popular natural remedy for dry skin ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Chamomilla recutita flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add the Chamomilla recutita flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the flowers.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Complementary therapies in medicine, Chamomilla recutita teas do not appear to be effective for treating dry skin, as none of the studies found positive effects for treating skin disorders with chamomile.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Chamomilla recutita teas can be an effective treatment for dry skin ailments, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as redness and itching, or interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is essential to start with a small dose and monitor your skin's reaction, and avoid consuming it if you have a known allergy to ragweed or other plants in the same family.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat dry skin ailments, including eczema and psoriasis. The soothing properties of aloe vera teas help to calm and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents of aloe vera teas, including vitamins A, C, and E, as well as aloe-emodin and aloe-queritin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to soothe and protect the skin. Regular consumption of aloe vera teas can provide long-term benefits, including improved skin hydration, reduced redness and itchiness, and enhanced skin elasticity.
preparation_procedure: Take 2 cups of water and boil it in a pot.
Wash 2-3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and remove any yellow sap.
Add the aloe vera gel to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Steep for 10-15 minutes then strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink the aloe vera tea while warm, 1-2 times a day to soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Infectious disorders drug targets, Aloe vera teas for dry skin may be beneficial as part of a natural remedy due to the potential therapeutic properties of Aloe vera in treating various skin ailments, including skin conditions such as dry skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be used to treat dry skin ailments due to its soothing and moisturizing properties, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. To use Aloe vera teas safely, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it if you have any underlying health conditions, and be cautious when taking it with other herbal remedies or supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, derived from the elderberry plant, have been traditionally used to treat dry skin ailments, such as eczema and dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote collagen production, thereby improving skin elasticity and hydration. Regular consumption of Sambucus nigra teas has been found to provide benefits in treating dry skin ailments, including reduced inflammation, improved skin texture, and enhanced overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the flowers and transfer the tea to a clean cup. Add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and moisturize dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Sambucus nigra teas for dry skin are not specifically mentioned in the provided study, but it is reported that information on nine plants used for managing dry skin emerged from this study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to treat dry skin ailments, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other active compounds. To use Sambucus nigra teas safely, take precautions by starting with small amounts, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding use if you have sensitive skin or are taking medications for blood thinning or diabetes, as well as being cautious when consuming with other herbs or supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, also known as oat straw tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry skin ailments due to their soothing and moisturizing properties. The polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in Avena sativa teas help to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and promote skin elasticity, making it an effective remedy for dry skin. The beta-glucans in Avena sativa teas also stimulate the production of hyaluronic acid, which helps to retain moisture in the skin and provide long-lasting hydration. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas can help to improve skin texture, reduce dryness, and alleviate symptoms associated with dry skin conditions, such as itchiness and irritation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried Avena sativa with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste and to help lock in moisture.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Avena sativa tea per day, ideally before bedtime, to help soothe dry skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Infectious disorders drug targets, Avena sativa teas for dry skin may be beneficial for skin health, but the provided study does not specifically mention Avena sativa teas as a treatment for dry skin, it only discusses the general benefits of herbal remedies for skin infections and diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat dry skin ailments, but using them may result in side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to their high saponin content. Precautions to take when using Avena sativa teas include avoiding excessive consumption, especially for individuals with sensitive stomachs or those taking medications, to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
